arguments.task: Open the official ClickUp API page and related first-party documentation needed to verify: 1) one-line product/API description, 2) all documented authentication methods for the API, 3) pricing or plan/access requirements for API use, 4) API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth, 5) whether ClickUp offers an official MCP server/integration, 6) rate limits. Read only live first-party pages that support these fields, preferring canonical docs, developer, auth, help, blog, and pricing pages. Return concise extracted findings with the exact supporting URLs actually visited and read. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for content to load and read the page text. Do not use search result snippets as evidence.

input.thought: Open and extract official ClickUp API, auth, pricing, and MCP evidence from first-party pages.

raw_result.description: ClickUp is an all-in-one productivity platform with a public API for managing workspace data and workflow automation.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe two API auth methods: Personal API Token for individual/testing use via the Authorization header, and OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for third-party apps exchanging client credentials/code for access tokens.
raw_result.gating_reason: ClickUp’s public API is available on all ClickUp plans, including lower tiers; however, some endpoints and parameters are limited to Business Plus or Enterprise plans.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with broad coverage across tasks, lists, folders, spaces, comments, custom fields, goals, guests, time tracking, views, webhooks, users, and workspace administration; v2/v3 documented with OpenAPI.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official developer docs include ClickUp's MCP Server documentation for LLM integrations.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate limits are plan-based: Free/Unlimited/Business 100 requests per minute, Business Plus 1,000 requests per minute, and Enterprise 10,000 requests per minute. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 and rate-limit headers including X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong first-party developer portal with dedicated authentication, OpenAPI, plan-availability, rate-limit, and official MCP documentation pages. API availability on all plans is a positive signal; some advanced admin/time-tracking features are plan-restricted rather than API-gated overall.
